evidence: Paper includes random forest/blockForest as prediction methods evaluated on 18 TCGA cancer datasets. In the Discussion, they note: "like the standard random forest variant, it does not yield easily interpretable models, even though the strengths of the variables can be assessed via the variable importance measure(s) output as a by-product of the random forest algorithms." However, they do not actually report, interpret, or rank features based on variable importance. All conclusions are based on prediction performance (ibrier and C-index), model sparsity, and computation time. Variable importance is mentioned only to acknowledge why RF models lack interpretability, not as a basis for conclusions.
